text_fr: l  'Assemblée généra/e,
text_en: the General Assembly,

text_fr: Rappelant ses résolutions 1874 (S-IV) du 27 juin 1963, 2006 (XIX) du  18  février 1965,  2053  A (XX) du  15  dé› cembre 1965, 2249 (S-V) du 23  mai  1967, 2308 (XXII) du 13  décembre 1967,  2451 (XXIII) du  19  déccmbrc 1968, 2670 (XXV) du 8 décembre 1970,  2835 (XXVI) du  17  dé› cembre 1971,  2965 (XXVII) du  13  décembrc 1972,  3091 (XXVlll) du  7 décembre 1973,  3239 (XXIX) du 29  no› vembre 1974, 3457 (XXX) du  JO  décembre 1975,  31/105 du  15  décembre 1976,  32/106  du  15  décembre 1977, 33/114 du  18  décembre 1978,  34/53  du  23  novembre 1979, 35/121 du 11  décembre 1980,  36/37 du  18  novem› bre  1981  et  37/93 du  10  décembre 1982,
text_en: Recalling its resolutions 1874 (S-IV) of 27 June 1963, 2006 (XIX) of 18 February 1965, 2053 A (XX) of 15 December 1965, 2249 (S-V) of 23 May 1967, 2308 (XXII) of 13 December 1967, 2451 (XXIII) of 19 December 1968, 2670 (XXV) of 8 December 1970, 2835 (XXVI) of 17 December 1971, 2965 (XXVII) of 13 December 1972, 3091 (XXVll) of 7 December 1973, 3239 (XXIX) of 29 November 1974, 3457 (XXX) of OJ December 1975, 31/105 of 15 December 1976, 32/106 of 15 December 1977, 33/114 of 18 December 1978, 34/53 of 23 November 1979, 35/121 of 11 December 1980, 36/37 of 18 November 1981 and 37/93 of 10 December 1982,

text_fr: Tenant compte des vues exprimées et  des points soule› vés, quant au probleme du maintien de la paix, au cours du débat consacré a la question,
text_en: Taking into account the views expressed and the points raised in connection with the question of peacekeeping during the debate on the item,

text_fr: Réaffirmant la  responsabilité principale  du  maintien de  la  paix  et  de  la  sécurité internationales conférée au Conseil de  sécurité,
text_en: Reaffirming the primary responsibility of the Security Council for the maintenance of international peace and security,

text_fr: Consciente du role capital joué par les forces de main› tien de la paix de l'Organisation des Nations Unies pour mettre en ceuvre les décisions du Conseil de sécurité dans l'exercice de sa responsabilité principale conformément a la Charle des  Nations Unies,
text_en: Aware of the vital role played by the peacekeeping forces of the United Nations in implementing the decisions of the Security Council in the exercise of its primary responsibility in accordance with the Charter of the United Nations,

text_fr: Reconnaissant que la présence des forces de maintien de la paix de l'Organisation des Nations Unies autorisées par  le  Conseil  de  sécurité dans  les  zones  de  conflit témoigne du souci commun des Membres de l'Organisa› tion  de  préserver la  stabilité et  d'atténuer les  tensions dans ces  régions,
text_en: Recognising that the presence of United Nations peacekeeping forces authorized by the Security Council in areas of conflict reflects the common concern of the Members of the Organization to preserve stability and alleviate tensions in those areas,

text_fr: Consciente de  la  situation  financiere  extremement difficile  des  forces  de  maintien de  la  paix de  l'Organisa› tion des  Nations Unies,  compte tenu du  lourd  fardeau supporté par les  pays qui  fournissent  des  troupes,  sur› tout les  pays en développement,
text_en: Aware of the extremely difficult financial situation of the United Nations Organization's peacekeeping forces, given the heavy burden borne by troop-contributing countries on all developing countries,

text_fr: Soulignant que les  Etats Membrcs ont, conformément a la  Charte,  la  responsabilité collect ive  de  supporter équitablement le  fardeau  financicr de, opérations enga› gées par le  Conseil  de  sécurité, qui  doivent  continuer a etre menées avec le  maximum d'efficacité et  d'éconornie,
text_en: Stressing that Member States have, in accordance with the Charter, the collective responsibility to bear fairly the financial burden of operations undertaken by the Security Council, which must continue to be carried out with maximum efficiency and economy,

text_fr: Demandant instamment, en meme temps, que l'on en› courage  l'apport d'une coopération et  d'un appui  aux forces  de  maintien de  la  paix de  l'Organisation des  Na› tions Unies dans d'autres dornaines,
text_en: Urging, at the same time, that there should be courage in providing cooperation and support to the United Nations peacekeeping forces in other areas,

text_fr: Reconnaissant la  nécessité d'arnéliorer la  capacité et l'efficacité des forces de maintien de  la  paix de  l'Organi› sation des  Nations Unies,
text_en: Recognizing the need to strengthen the capacity and effectiveness of the United Nations Organi peace-keeping forces,

text_fr: Rendant hommage au Secrétaire général pour la fa¡;on dont il s'acquitte des opérations de maintie11 de la paix de l'Organisation des Nations Unies décidées par le  Conseil de sécurité,
text_en: Commending the Secretary-General for the fact that he is carrying out the peace-keeping operations of the United Nations decided by the Security Council,

text_fr: Convaincue que les questions relatives au rnaintien de la paix  par l'Organisation des  Nations Unics  sont  d'unc
text_en: Convinced that issues relating to peacemaking by the United Nations are of unc

text_fr: importance  telle  que  l'Organisation doit  continuer  de s'employer a étudier !'ensemble de  toute la question des opérations de maintien de la paix sous tous leurs aspects,
text_en: of the whole question of peacekeeping operations in all their aspects,

text_fr: Prenant acte du rapport du Comité spécial des opéra› tions de  maintien de la  paix'",
text_en: Taking note of the report of the Special Committee on Peacekeeping Operations",

items_fr: 1. Se déclare convaincue que les opérations de main› tien  de  la  paix  de  l'Organisation des  Nations  Unies, menées avec  le  consentement  du  pays  h6te  et  dans  le respect de sa souveraineté et de son intégrité territoriale, conformément a la  Chane des  Nations Unies, représen› tent  une  fonction  essentielle  de  l'Organisation,  mais qu'elles ne doivent pas remplacer un reglement pacifique des  différends et  n'ont done qu'un caractere provisoire;
2. Demande  instamment a tous  les  intéressés de coopérer pleinement a l'exécution des  opérations de maintien de la paix de l'Organisation des Nations Unies;
3. Réajfirme  et  proruge le rnandat  conféré au Comité spécial des opérations de maintien de la paix par les  résolutions pertinente\ de  l'Assemblée générale;
4. Se  déclare préoccupée par la  situation financiere difficile des  opérations de maintien de la  paix de l'Orga› nisation  des  Nations Unic,;

5. Insiste de nouveau aupres du Comité spécial des opérations de maintien de la  paix pour que, conformé› ment a son  mandat,  il  redouble d'efforts pour achever l'élaboration des príncipes directeurs convenus qui régi› ront la conduite des opérations de maintien de la paix de l'Organisation des  Nations Unies,  en  application  de  la Charle des  Nations  Unies,  et  pour qu'il réexamine les aspects  précis du  déroulement pratique des  opérations de  maintien de la paix;

6. Prie le  Comité spécial des opérations de maintien de la paix de présenter un rapport d'activilé sur sa situa› tion actuelle,  de  déterminer les  secteurs ou des  progres sont  possibles et  ceux  ou ils  seraient  difficiles  ou conti› nueraient  a etre escomptés et  d'envisager des  proposi› tions tendant a relancer et  rationaliser ses  travaux;

7. Décide d'inscrire a l'ordre du jour provisoire de sa trente-neuvieme  session  la  question  intitulée «Etude d'ensemble de toute la question des opérations de main› tien  de la  paix  sous tous  leurs  aspects».
items_en: 1. Expresses its conviction that the peace-keeping operations of the United Nations, conducted with the consent of the host country and with respect for its sovereignty and territorial integrity, in accordance with the United Nations Chane, represent an essential function of the Organization, but must not replace a peaceful settlement of disputes and have only a provisional character;
2. Urges all concerned to cooperate fully in the implementation of United Nations peacekeeping operations;
3. Reaffirms and proruches the mandate of the Special Committee on Peacekeeping Operations under the relevant General Assembly resolutions;
4. Expresses its concern at the difficult financial situation of the peacekeeping operations of the Orga› nization of the United Nations,

5. Reiterates its call to the Special Committee on Peacekeeping Operations to intensify its efforts to complete the development of the agreed guidelines governing the conduct of United Nations peacekeeping operations pursuant to the United Nations Charle and to review the specific aspects of the practical conduct of peacekeeping operations;

6. Requests the Special Committee on Peacekeeping Operations to submit an activity report on its current situation, to identify sectors or progresses are possible and those which would be difficult or unanticipated and to consider proposals for restarting and rationalizing its work;
7. Decides to include in the provisional agenda of its thirty-ninth session the item entitled "Comprehensive review of the whole question of peacekeeping operations in all their aspects".

1. Calls once again upon Member States and United Nations bodies and agencies, as well as other international organizations, to govern and to provide greater financial resources, as well as more personnel, technical equipment and training, to contribute to the Programme; '

8. Notes with appreciation the progress made in the framework of the Global Satellite Information Dissemination and Exchange Project, which the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization is carrying out with the cooperation of INTELSAT and INTERSPOUTNIK and the regional radio associations› broadcasting from Africa, Asia, Europe and the Arab States and with the support of the Programme;

9. Acknowledges that very few countries have responded to Resolution 4/22 on the reduction of telecommunications services for the exchange of information, adopted by the General Conference of the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization at its twenty-first session", and calls once again on Member States to respond positively and effectively to this resolution;

10. Reaffirms its strong support for the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization and its efforts to establish a new world information and communication order, as well as its second medium-term plan for 1984-1989 and its efforts to stimulate research to meet the needs created by the accelerated progress of technology and the growing role of social and cultural communication!

11. Requests the Director-General of the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization to continue his efforts in the field of information and communication and to submit to the General Assembly at its thirty-ninth session a detailed report on the implementation of the Programme and on activities relating to the establishment of a new world information and communication order and, in cooperation with the International Telecommunications Union, on the social and cultural effects of the accelerated development of communication techniques.

text_en: Taking note of the Geneva Declaration on Palestine and the Programme of Action for the Realization of the Rights of the Palestinians, adopted unanimously by the International Conference on the Question of Palestine,' in particular the Programme of Action '11 D,

text_fr: Consciente qu'il faut que tous les  pays, le  systeme des Nations  Unies  dans  son  ensemble  et  toutes  les  autres parties  intéressées collaborent a l'instauration  d'un nouvel ordre mondial de l'information et  de la commu› nication  fondé, notamment,  sur  la  libre  circulation  et une diffusion plus large et mieux équilibrée de l'informa› tion,  qui garantisse  la  diversité des  sources d'informa› tion et le  libre acces a l'information, et,  en  particulier, qu'il est  urgent  de  mettre  un  terme a l'état de  dépen› dance des  pays  en  développement dans le  domaine de l'information et  de  la  communication,  le  principe  de l'égalité souveraine  des  nations  s'étendant aussi a ce domaine, et que ce nouvel ordre doit également contri› buer a renforcer la paix et  la compréhension internatio› nale, a permettre a tous de participer effectivement a la vie  politique, économique, sociale et  culturelle et a pro› mouvoir  la  compréhension et  l'amitié entre  toutes  les nations ainsi que les  droits de l'homme,
text_en: Aware of the need for all countries, the United Nations system as a whole and all other interested parties to cooperate in the establishment of a new world information and communication order based, inter alia, on the free movement and wider and more balanced dissemination of information, which guarantees the diversity of sources of information and free access to information, and in particular the urgent need to put an end to the state of depenance of developing countries in the field of information and communication, the principle of the sovereign equality of nations also extending to this field, and that this new order must also contribute to strengthening international peace and understanding, to enable all to participate effectively in political, economic, social and cultural life and to promote understanding and friendship among all nations and human rights,

text_fr: Réajfirmant que  l'instauration  d'un  nouvel  ordre mondial de l'information et de la communication est liée au nouvel ordre économique international et  fait  partie intégrante du  processus  de  développement  interna› tional,
text_en: Reaffirming that the establishment of a new world information and communication order is linked to the new international economic order and is an integral part of the international development process,

text_fr: Soulignant le  role  important  que joue l'information pour ce  qui est  de  promouvoir la  compréhension et  le soutien  de  l'instauration du  nouvel  ordre économique international et de la coopération internationale pour le développement,
text_en: Emphasizing the important role of information in promoting understanding and support for the establishment of the new international economic order and international cooperation for development,

text_fr: Soulignant le  role que l'information joue dans la pro› motion  du  désarmement universel  et  dans  la  prise  de conscience accrue,  par un public aussi  vaste que possi› ble,  du  rapport  qui  existe  entre  le  désarmement et  le développement,
text_en: Underlining the role that information plays in the pro› motion for universal disarmament and in increasing awareness, by an audience as wide as possible, of the relationship between disarmament and development,

text_fr: Réajfirmant le  role  primordial que I'Assemblée géné› rale doit jouer en ce qui concerne l'élaboration, la coor› dination et  l'harmonisation des politiques et activités de l'Organisation des  Nations  Unies  dans  le  domaine  de l'information et  reconnaissant  le  role central et  impor› tant de l'Organisation des  Nations Unies  pour l'éduca› tion, la science et  la culture dans le  domaine de l'infor› mation et de la communication,
text_en: Reaffirming the essential role which the General Assembly must play in the development, coordination and harmonization of United Nations policies and activities in the field of information and recognizing the central and important role of the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization in the field of information and communication,

text_fr: Soulignant la  complémentarité des  activités dans  le domaine de  l'information et  de la  communication et  la nécessité de rcnforcer la  coopération et  la  coordination entre  les organes,  organisations  et organismes  des Nations Unie, s'occupant de divers aspects de l'informa› tion et  de la  communication,

Exprime sa satisfaction des  travaux de  la  Table ronde sur  l'instauration d'un nouvel  ordre  mondial  de l'information  et de la communication,  organiséc conjointement  par l'Organisation des  Nations  Unies  et l'Organisation des  Nations  Unies  pour  l'éducation, la science  et  la  culture a Innsbruck  (Autriche)  du  14  au 19  septembre  1983,  et  du  rapport  qui  en  est  issu";

17. Prie le  Secrétaire général de  veiller  a ce  que  le Département de l'information convoque, conjointement avec l'Organisation des  Nations Unies pour l'éducation, la  science  et  la  culture,  une  deuxieme  table  ronde  en 1985  pour examiner de  facon  plus  détaillée les  progres accomplis sur la voie de l'instauration d'un nouvel ordre mondial de l'information et  de la communication, table ronde a laquelle participeraient des  journalistes profes› sionnels,  des  décideurs  et  des  chercheurs  dans  les diverses disciplines en  cause, ainsi que des représentants des organes d'information internationaux et  des organi› sations et  associations professionnelles;

18. Prie le  Secrétaire général de  rendre  compte au Comité de  l'information,  lors  de  sa  session  de  1984 consacrée aux  questions  de  fond,  de  l'application  de toutes les  recommandations  formulées dans  le  rapport du  Comité;

19. Prie le  Secrétaire général de faire rapport a l'As› semblée générale, lors de sa trente-neuvieme session, sur l'application de  la  présente résolution, en particulier de l'application de  toutes  les  recommandations  formulées dans l'annexe a la  présente résolution;
20. Prie le  Comité de  l'information de faire  rapport a  l'Assemblée générale lors  de  sa trente-neuvieme session;
21. Décide d'inscrire a l'ordre du jour provisoire de sa  trente-neuvieme session  la  question  intitulée «Ques› tions relatives a l'information».
items_en: 6. Requests that the Joint Committee on Information of the United Nations, as an essential instrument for inter-agency cooperation and coordination in the field of information, be strengthened and made more effective, and that its secretariat develop new working methods, indicative planning and longer-term joint action, in particular with a view to promoting a new world information and communication order;

7. Reaffirms the importance of the increasing role played by United Nations public information programmes in promoting public understanding of and support for the activities of the Organization, and requests the Secretary-General to continue to consider, in cooperation with the Joint Inspection Unit, the current activities of the Department of Public Information with a view to ensuring better and more efficient use of the resources available to it;

8. Requests the Department of Public Information, through its training programmes, to contribute more effectively to the development of human resources, capacities and technical resources of the information media of developing countries;

9. Requests the Secretary-General, as a matter of urgency, to take steps, within the framework of the next programme budget, to ensure that I Unite des Carai'bes of the Radio Service of the Department of Public Information can address the positive programme of work indicated by the Secretary-General in his report on this issue", in particular by inaugurating a comprehensive programme in French and Creole and a limited programme in Dutch and Papiamento;

10. Requests the Secretary-General to take the necessary measures to ensure that regional television magazines are produced for national television organizations, upon request, and that they are broadcast on a regular basis, taking into account the priorities set by the General Assembly;
11. Invites the Department of Public Information, in the light of the request of the Government of Indonesia, to reopen, as a priority measure, the United Nations Information Centre in Jakarta;

12. Invites the Department of Public Information to respond favourably to the request of the Government of the United Republic of Cameroon to force the United Nations Information Centre in Yaoundé and to appoint a full-time Director there;

13. Invites the Department of Public Information to respond favourably to the request of the Government of the Republic of Burundi to strengthen the United Nations Information Centre in Bujumbura and to appoint a full-time Director there;

14. Invites the Committee on Transnational Companies, when exchanging information with the Committee on Information, as called for in the recommendation 21 of the Committee annexed to the present resolution, to draw the Committee's attention to the documents prepared by the Secretariat which have a report on the terms of reference of the Committee, in particular the documents of the United Nations Centre on Transnatio nal Companies, upon their consideration by the Commission, as well as the comments made by the Commission on those documents, on the understanding that care must be taken to avoid any overlap or duplication between the work of the two intergovernmental bodies;

15. Requests the Department of Public Information to ensure adequate publicity for policies and practices that violate the principles of international law relating to the occupation of war, in particular the Geneva Convention relative to the Protection of Civilian Persons in Time of War, of 12 aofü 1949, 3 " , in whatever way they are implemented, including policies and practices that impede the realization and exercise of the inalienable and national legitimate rights of the Palestinian people as provided for in the relevant resolutions of the United Nations;

16. Expresses its appreciation for the work of the Round Table on the Establishment of a New World Information and Communication Order, organized jointly by the United Nations and the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization in Innsbruck, Austria, from 14 to 19 September 1983, and the report thereof";

17. Requests the Secretary-General to ensure that the Department of Public Information, in conjunction with the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization, convenes a second round table in 1985 to discuss in more detail progress towards the establishment of a new world information and communication order, with the participation of professional journalists, policy makers and researchers in the various disciplines concerned, as well as representatives of international information bodies and professional organizations and associations;

18. Requests the Secretary-General to report to the Committee on Information at its substantive session in 1984 on the implementation of all the recommendations contained in the report of the Committee;

19. Requests the Secretary-General to report to the General Assembly at its thirty-ninth session on the implementation of the present resolution, in particular on the implementation of all the recommendations contained in the annex to the present resolution;
20. Requests the Committee on Information to report to the General Assembly at its thirty-ninth session;
21. Decides to include in the provisional agenda of its thirty-ninth session the item entitled "Information-related matters".

The forty-three recommendations of the Information Committee approved by the General Assembly in its resolution 37/94 B of IO December 1982, and all the provisions of that resolution, are reaffirmed.The recommendations which have not yet been implemented should be fully taken into account in the light of the views expressed by delegations at the 100th plenary meeting of the Thirty-Eptieth Session of the Assembly on IO December 1982.

2. The mandate of the Committee on Information, as set out in General Assembly resolution 34/182 of 18 December 1979 and reaffirmed by the Assembly in its resolutions 35/201 of 16 December 1980, 36/149 of 16 December 1981 and 37/94 of 10 December 1982, should be renewed.

PROMOTION OF THE ESTABLISHMENT OF A NEW WORLD INFORMATION AND COMMUNICATION ORDER WITH MORE JUST AND EFFICIENCY› CACE, DESTINED ' STRENGTHENING PEACE AND INTERNAL COMPREHENSION› NATIONAL AND FREE MOVEMENT AND MORE LAROE AND MIDDLE INFORMATION

3. All countries, the United Nations system as a whole and all other interested parties should cooperate in the establishment of a new world information and communication order, based, inter alia, on the free circulation and wider and more balanced difusion of information, which guarantees the diversity of sources of information and free access to information; in particular, there is an urgent need to put an end to the state of dependence of developing countries in the field of information and communication, the principle of the sovereign equality of nations extending also to this field; this new order must also contribute to strengthening peace and international understanding, enabling all parties to participate in political, economic, social and cultural life, and to promote understanding and friendship among all nations and human rights.

4. The media around the world should be called upon to support the international community's action in the field of global development and, in particular, the Elforts that developing countries are doing to ensure their own economic, social and cultural progress!

.5. All United Nations agencies should be called upon to contribute, in a concerted manner, through their information services, to the promotion of United Nations development activities, in particular those aimed at improving the living conditions of the people of developing countries.

6. The purpose of these appeals should be to provide a more comprehensive and realistic picture of the activities and possibilities for action of the United Nations system in the follow-up to the objectives set out in the Charter of the United Nations.

7. It is important to note the need to create a climate of confidence in relations between States in order to ease tensions, and it would be desirable, in this regard, to appeal to all States and to all media to contribute to the promotion of the objectives of strengthening peace and understanding.

8. Reaffirming the central role that the General Assembly must play in the development, coordination and harmonization of United Nations policies and activities in the field of information and recognizing the central and important role of the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization in the field of information and communication, the Committee on Information considers that cooperation between the United Nations and the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization with a view to promoting the establishment of a new information order and communication should be more regular, particularly at the level of working relations, so as to maximize the contribution of the Department of Public Information to the Elforts of the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization.

9. The Department of Public Information should also disseminate as soon as possible information on the activities of the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) in connection with the establishment of a new world information and communication order on the work of the Committee on Information in the field.

10. 12. The Secretary-General should present to the General Assembly at its thirty-eighth session the report of the Round Table on the establishment of a new world information and communication order, which was held in lnnsbruck, Austria, from 14 to 19 September 1983."

13. It is agreed to call upon the United Nations system as a whole and the developed countries to cooperate in a concerted effort to strengthen the communication infrastructure of developing countries in accordance with the priorities given to these areas by the developing countries.

14. The Departemmenl of Information should further strengthen its cooperation relations with the Pool of Non-Aligned Press Agencies, as well as with the regional news agencies of the countries ,·11 development, this cooperation being a concrete step towards a fairer and more equitable world market for information ,1 thus contributing to the establishment of a new world information and information order .

15. The United Nations system, in particular the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization, should step up its efforts to provide developing countries with all the necessary assistance, taking into account their interests the information needs and the measures already adopted, within the framework of the United Nations system, including in part
(a) Assistance to developing countries for the training of journalists and technical staff and the creation of appropriate teaching and research facilities;

(b) The granting to developing countries of conditions of access, favourable to communicative techniques, they need to establish a national information and communicative network11 that responds to the specific needs of the country concerned;

(e) Creating the conditions for developing countries to progressively produce the communication technologies that meet their needs, as well as the necessary programmes, including radio and television, using their own resources;
(d) Assistance for the establishment of telecommunications networks at the subregional, regional and interregional levels, including 111 among developing countries, without preconditions.

16. All public information activities of the Department of Public Information should be based on the principles of the Charle des Nations, the United Nations and the desire to establish a new world information and communication order and to be undertaken with these principles and aspirations, and should reflect the consensus reached by States in resolutions 4/19, 4/21 and 4/22 adopted by the General Conference of the Organization of, Natinn,
